Hope to see you again soon 🙏🙏","reply_language":"en","reply_posted_at_unix_micros":1766250910000000,"reply_updated_at_unix_micros":1766250910000000,"published_at":"2025-12-08T19:36:21.149963Z"},{"Name":"Viale M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK1fZt7b-WG0GIpQeuoB2NMU8F7uYMrYq0RGj0FOC2trwmywQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"**Have a cup, but leave the beans on the shelf.**

Taf Coffee seems to be suffering from an identity crisis. As a coffee shop, it is undistinguished from hundreds of others in Athens: pleasant, cozy, and enveloped in cigarette smoke.

As a roastery, it offers a staggering selection of coffee, predominantly from the Americas with a minority from Ethiopia. However, there is virtually nothing from Asia—no Indonesia (not even Java), no Vietnam, and none of the glorious coffee of India. This geographical gap could be excused if the American selection were consistently exceptional. Unfortunately, the sheer breadth of the selection results in a mixed experience. The flavor profiles are hard to decipher, with adjectives that seem drawn from the wild imagination of the roaster on any given day.

The staff, hurried by customers waiting for their espresso, was unhelpful and could offer little in the way of guidance or advice regarding the beans. To make matters worse, prices are exceptionally high, ranging from €50 to €150 per kilogram. At this price level, experimentation becomes very expensive, very quickly. Our best advice: come here for a cup of coffee, but leave the beans on the shelves.

As ordinary consumers, we are not paid or compensated in any way for this review. We aim to provide fair and reliable feedback for fellow coffee enthusiasts.","Images":null,"When":"5 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tKWFRsVk5WelV3TmtaTWVFY3lPV1JSUm01VWNuYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":3,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/111417072343809195680/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1767346343714444,"updated_at_unix_micros":1767346343714444,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"**Have a cup, but leave the beans on the shelf.**

Taf Coffee seems to be suffering from an identity crisis. As a coffee shop, it is undistinguished from hundreds of others in Athens: pleasant, cozy, and enveloped in cigarette smoke.

As a roastery, it offers a staggering selection of coffee, predominantly from the Americas with a minority from Ethiopia. However, there is virtually nothing from Asia—no Indonesia (not even Java), no Vietnam, and none of the glorious coffee of India. This geographical gap could be excused if the American selection were consistently exceptional. Unfortunately, the sheer breadth of the selection results in a mixed experience. The flavor profiles are hard to decipher, with adjectives that seem drawn from the wild imagination of the roaster on any given day.

The staff, hurried by customers waiting for their espresso, was unhelpful and could offer little in the way of guidance or advice regarding the beans. To make matters worse, prices are exceptionally high, ranging from €50 to €150 per kilogram. At this price level, experimentation becomes very expensive, very quickly. Our best advice: come here for a cup of coffee, but leave the beans on the shelves.

As ordinary consumers, we are not paid or compensated in any way for this review. We aim to provide fair and reliable feedback for fellow coffee enthusiasts.","text_translated":"","reply_text_original":"Thank you for taking the time to share your review.

We are a roastery with a clear focus first and foremost on quality. For this reason, we do not compromise by offering coffees simply to claim representation from all over the world, this has never been our goal. Every coffee we offer must meet the quality and ethical standards we have set.

Our entire project is built on personal, long-term relationships with producers. We do not source coffees from “trendy” origins, but work with long-standing partners through the Taf Direct Relationship program. These relationships require time and effort to build and maintain, and when the right opportunity arises and we are ready, we will expand the number of origins within the Taf Direct Relationship program.

The prices of our retail beans reflect this commitment to quality, as well as our commitment to ensuring that the producers we collaborate with are compensated fairly. At the same time, there is a wide range of prices available on our shelves, designed to accommodate different preferences and levels of exploration.

Regarding our cup profiles, they are the result of standardized processes defined by specialty coffee industry protocols, carried out by a certified Q Grader in our certified lab. They are intended both for those familiar with the sensory aspect of coffee and for consumers who wish to learn and explore. That said, we understand that a busy coffee shop environment is not always the ideal setting for this to be fully communicated or appreciated.

Our staff is there to guide guests through their tasting experience, and this is the type of visit we strive to offer. However, as a coffee shop is a lively and constantly moving space, there are moments when it can be more challenging for the team to fully meet this role. Nevertheless, this is something we actively work on, as we aim to continuously improve.

Regarding the cigarette smoke mentioned, this of course refers to the outdoor seating area. Unfortunately, this is beyond our control, as smoking is legally permitted in outdoor spaces.

We are glad you enjoyed your experience inside the shop. Our retail shelf is carefully curated for those who value origin transparency, quality, skilled roasting, consistency, and conscious sourcing — and for all these reasons, we believe our coffees are worth taking home.

Thank you,
Taf Coffee Team ","reply_language":"en","reply_posted_at_unix_micros":1767620478000000,"reply_updated_at_unix_micros":1767620478000000,"published_at":"2026-01-02T09:32:23.714444Z"},{"Name":"Catherine McCandless","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKO554ooplZuD3Uu-7d70GAvVOOV7Q8hUVT9bkofFxH5yLMMg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Delicious coffee! [{"Name":"Monica Podby","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLCXy_LuAnxJTFfmNa7FSkjSjeDEwopeTdJ-T4P3Ies6SQF40H=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Being obsessed with coffee I am very picky when finding the right place to grab some. I am not a big fan of specialty coffee as I know from experience that it is often not good.

Came here as I was intrigued this was a stop on many food tours and ate here twice in 2.5 days. I see why it's part of a foodie tour!

The phyllo is hand-made, incredible and what makes this the best in town.  Crisp and shatter thin but not delicate where it flakes apart. Not greasy. Not soft and croissant like. The right ratio of spinach, feta and herbs. The Bougatsa is great as well. It comes out of a heated display case piping hot and he'll cut it for you if you eat it there. We bought one to go and carried it to the airport. Three hours later it was still warm with the crispy phyllo!

The coffees are excellent and his cappuccino is the best we've had as well. Super smooth, creamy and not bitter.

The owner is friendly and warm. The second time we came, he wished my husband a good day and patted him on the arm in such a nice way.

There are three bakeries just a couple of doors away from each other on this street. This is the smallest and so low key that you might walk past it but this is the best one.

Just walk on up from Monastiriki Square or down from the Central market and you'll find him. Gray walls and small display case. [{"Name":"Antje-Kathrin Schumann","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVugv_hm6xB6TU_dUkmf4cR9UtYAXRcR33MCdFUi_lSeswcL2Qx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you want a really good coffee to go between two meetings or a shopping marathon to recharge your batteries, then this is the place to go.

👉 Tate Speciality Coffee

📍 Kolokotrani 25, 10562 Athens, Greece

It is in the city center.

☕️ Nice latte macchiato.
